title: io.net
summary: io.net is a decentralized AI infrastructure platform for GPU clusters, agent hosting, and confidential inference. Arcium currently presents it as part of the encrypted AI ecosystem rather than a clearly documented Arcium integration on the public product side.
description:
io.net describes itself as an open-source AI infrastructure platform with on-demand GPU clusters in 130+ countries, flexible deployment for containers and Ray clusters, and up to 70% cost savings compared with major centralized clouds. Its docs now also cover agent frameworks, AI APIs, and confidential inference.

Arcium includes io.net in its AI ecosystem coverage. That supports Atlas inclusion, but I did not find a current io.net product page naming a specific Arcium-powered feature.

bodySections.title: Platform_Surface
bodySections.body: The official site and docs position io.net as a decentralized compute and AI platform spanning GPU cluster provisioning, AI APIs, agent frameworks, and confidential inference. Public documentation also emphasizes Solana roots and developer-facing deployment surfaces.

bodySections.title: Arcium_Relevance
bodySections.body: Arcium lists io.net among AI ecosystem partners. That is enough to keep io.net in Atlas as an ecosystem project, but not enough to classify the relationship as a confirmed Arcium integration without a project-side implementation page.

statusNote: Arcium includes io.net in its AI ecosystem coverage, but I did not find a current io.net page describing an explicitly Arcium-powered workflow.
